author: @BlueJeebs
text: Great work! Linking 5-10 of these in series and connecting it under your wind turbine  design with a gear switch to generate high output during high wind/storm scenarios might work! I also wonder if that casing could be 3D printed for those holes lining up as you mentioned for higher efficiency.

author: @SimonPlatten
text: You are a very clever man, really enjoying watching your progress!  I want to get off grid, I have quite a large area of land, I'm currently planning on installing Solar with Tesla batteries, already have 4KW of Solar on my roof, which I benefit form the FIT.

replies.author: @DT-dc4br
replies.text: If you're aiming to go off-grid & already have PV, then it might be a good candidate for a ground-sourced heat pump (especially if you're friends with anyone who has access to a digger to take the sting out of the ground works cost).  I've had 4kw of PV since 2014; sourced the panels, a roofer & a certified electrician to sign it off - all in it cost about half of what "one stop shops" were quoting so it paid for itself in <6yrs.   Not much land here, but have 5m^2 of solar thermal panels, which I'm hoping to use to charge a heat store, then run a heat pump from that.

replies.author: @SimonPlatten
replies.text: @DT-dc4br we have air source heat pump already.  Waiting for quotation for new solar system and batteries.

author: @briscaf1stockcar
text: I've followed you and others for a few years now. On battery stuff ( kev carbonator?, cyrex2? <-- probably spelt wrong ) and others. All though you guys have been amazing on battery tech. ( I honestly get confused over some of the stuff you say! ) should we be considering old tech like the flywheel in combination with new tech? Because you seem to be asking questions about mechanical systems as well? A few of you videos about vibration generation and mechanical systems have got me thinking.

author: @claytonmccormick622
text: ok you got 50 volts a good start how many amps or milliamps ? even small projects added up can make a contribution freeing yourself from the grid and remember they do not all have to make electricity pumping water up hill lifting weights making heat also work and can be made to be interchangeable one of the  cheapest ways to store power is  pumped hydro. for instance solar concentration can create enough heat to be stored for use over night or off peak power can move enough heat to heat or cool for use during peak usage i always find waching you fun as you think about a lot of things i had not though about have you though about using all your little prodects to heat a tank of water or some such that can add to your comfort without adding to the bills. my though that with all these reality shows it would be fun to take real small towns in the poorer parts of the world and compete over fixing their problems.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no idea mate - but it was an easy project and a simple build - why not give it a go?

replies.author: @claytonmccormick622
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering yes and that is the kind of project that will get built but so many projects are to big to complicated to small i really want to see people building things that make a difference because  no one else  is going to help and things are going to get messy soon.

author: @Charlie-Oooooo
text: @Robert Murray-Smith Another great video Robert!!! Wouldn't it be cool to see the whole magnetic field created with your arrangement. One question: could you give us a quick current reading with the 30 volts (or if/when you move the coils closer)? Just curious about the ballpark wattage. Thanks!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure mate - I am guessing milliamps though

replies.author: @Charlie-Oooooo
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering milli amps, hmm. still is pretty cool for what you got there! higher rpms like a tesla boundary layer turbine (10k to 20k rpms, for example) would probably up the the juice, right? but that's a whole new ball game.  anyway, thank you kindly!!!

author: @totherarf
text:
If you are looking to increase efficiency you could do worse than having each coil lined up with each "bar" round the magnet. This would give you more of a sine wave output rather than  ripple as each coil squirts it's energy in and has some absorbed by the others! 
Then you could put a ferrous core in the coil and link it to the next via a band around the outside. this should give you a 10,000:1 improvement of the flux (minus the gaps)!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate
